LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION IS DENIED. IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION, BASED ON THE ANALYSIS IN BAEZ V. BOYLE & CO. (1995) 40 CAL.APP.4TH 700, 717, THAT JURISDICTION IS DETERMINED AT THE TIME OF EXPOSURE, NOT WHEN THE CAUSE OF ACTION ACCRUED. AT THAT TIME, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A BASIS TO ASSERT GENERAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. AS TO SPECIAL JURISDICTION, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF'S EXPOSURE OCCURRED IN WISCONSIN AND THAT THERE IS NOT A BASIS TO ASSERT SPECIAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. SEE CASSIER MINING CORP. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 66 CAL.APP.4TH 550, 555. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Dec-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.'S Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.
APPEARANCES: Matthew B. Lee, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, LLP 898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; Catherine M. Reichhold, Esq. of Armstrong & Associates, LLP 510/433-1830 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE MOTION IS DENIED. IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION, BASED ON THE ANALYSIS IN BAEZ V. BOYLE & CO. (1995) 40 CAL.APP.4TH 700, 717, THAT JURISDICTION IS DETERMINED AT THE TIME OF EXPOSURE, NOT WHEN THE CAUSE OF ACTION ACCRUED. AT THAT TIME, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A BASIS TO ASSERT GENERAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. AS TO SPECIAL JURISDICTION, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF'S EXPOSURE OCCURRED IN WISCONSIN AND THAT THERE IS NOT A BASIS TO ASSERT SPECIAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. SEE CASSIER MINING CORP. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 66 CAL.APP.4TH 550, 555. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
